response: The award Alex Rodriguez won two of is the Commissioner's Historic Achievement Award, which is sponsored by the Commissioner of Baseball. This award is presented by the Commissioner at his discretion and is not an annual award. The trophy is a gold baseball sitting atop a cylindrical silver base, created by Tiffany & Co. The award has been presented fourteen times by Commissioner Bud Selig: eleven times to players, once to a team, and twice to a non-player. The most recent recipient is Derek Jeter (2014), who was honored in the final year of his career for being "one of the most accomplished shortstops of all-time."
